rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Leave To File Proposed First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 26, 2021 line 2. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON's Motion For Leave To File Proposed First Amended Complaint is DENIED. On February 10, 2021 Plaintiff's Ex-parte Application to calendar a Motion for Leave to Amend was granted; and the motion was calendared for February 16, 2021. Per February 16, 2021 tentative ruling/minute order the motion was continued as follows: "Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to Amend Complaint is continued to March 19, 2021. By no later than March 12, 2021 Plaintiff to file a proposed amended complaint that alleges causes of action in clear and concise language with facts supporting each element of each cause of action. Each cause of action shall clearly indicate the defendant against whom the cause of action is asserted. Proposed amended complaint shall comply with all prior orders in this case including the order on Motions for Summary Adjudication, Discovery Orders and prior orders on motions for leave to amend. While not required, the Court suggests that the proposed amended complaint include relevant facts within each cause of action (as opposed to stating all facts in the "General" section and incorporating those facts into each cause of action). This organization will promote efficiency and will ensure that relevant facts in support of each element of each cause of action are properly asserted avoiding future unnecessary motion practice." Plaintiff failed to make the required filings and failed to appear on March 19, 2021. As of March 19, 2021, motion for terminating sanctions filed by defendant Ma has already been granted. On April 1, 2021 Plaintiff filed this motion. Attached to the motion is a proposed 84-page First Amended Complaint that still names Ma as a defendant and does not comply with the February 16, 2021 minute order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
